skill.name: nosql-expert
skill.description: Expert guidance for designing scalable NoSQL data models for Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and DynamoDB workloads.
skill.summary: Guides query-first NoSQL modeling, partition design, denormalization, and hot partition avoidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 39 potential issues, but review found they are false positives in Markdown database examples. No executable scripts, shell invocation, network code, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Distributed NoSQL schemas fail when teams model entities before queries. This skill guides query-first design for Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and DynamoDB systems.
actual_capabilities: Explains query-first modeling for Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and DynamoDB.
Guides partition key selection to reduce hot partition risk.
Compares SQL modeling with distributed NoSQL modeling tradeoffs.
Describes DynamoDB single-table design and adjacency list patterns.
Provides a checklist for access patterns, cardinality, growth, and consistency.
limitations: Does not connect to databases or inspect live production metrics.
Does not generate migration scripts or deploy database infrastructure.
Focuses on Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and DynamoDB instead of all NoSQL systems.
Requires users to provide expected queries, traffic patterns, and consistency needs.

use_cases.title: Plan a New High-Scale Schema
use_cases.description: Map core queries to partition keys, sort keys, indexes, and denormalized tables before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Partition and Index Choices
use_cases.description: Check whether access patterns create hot partitions, unbounded partitions, or inefficient scans.

use_cases.title: Choose a NoSQL Data Strategy
use_cases.description: Compare Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and DynamoDB design implications for a service workload.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Access Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: List the access patterns for this feature and explain which ones need direct NoSQL lookups.

prompt_templates.title: Design Partition Keys
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest partition keys and sort keys for these access patterns, then explain hot partition risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Proposed Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Cassandra or DynamoDB schema for query coverage, cardinality, partition growth, and consistency tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Denormalized Reads
prompt_templates.prompt: Design denormalized read models for these queries, including update consistency risks and index alternatives.

output_examples.input: I need to fetch a user profile and recent orders by user.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend a user-centered access pattern, a high-cardinality partition key, and an order-based sort strategy.

output_examples.input: Our table uses status as the partition key and traffic is uneven.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag status as low cardinality and suggest a key design that spreads reads across users or entities.

output_examples.input: We need alternate lookups by email and by user id.
output_examples.output: The skill would explain when to duplicate records, use a secondary index, or create another query-specific table.

best_practices: Start with complete access patterns before choosing tables, keys, or indexes.
Use high-cardinality partition keys and check for unbounded partition growth.
Document consistency expectations for every denormalized write path.
anti_patterns: Do not port a relational schema directly into Cassandra or DynamoDB.
Do not use low-cardinality values as primary partition keys for high-traffic data.
Do not rely on scans or filtering for production read paths.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and does not execute queries or connect to databases.

faq.question: Which databases does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Apache Cassandra, ScyllaDB, and Amazon DynamoDB design patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with DynamoDB single-table design?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains single-table access patterns, adjacency lists, keys, and index tradeoffs.

faq.question: Can it detect hot partitions?
faq.answer: It can review proposed keys and identify likely hot partition risks from the provided workload.

faq.question: Does it replace a database performance test?
faq.answer: No. It supports design review, but production workloads still need measurement and testing.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide entities, access patterns, expected volume, read and write rates, and consistency requirements.
